description_long: This object consists of a group of seven irregularly shaped rocks, each with a rugged, natural appearance and a coarse, granular surface texture. The rocks vary in size, with the largest being broad and somewhat flat, while the smallest are more rounded and compact. Their coloration is predominantly a muted gray-brown, with subtle variations in shading that suggest weathering and exposure to the elements. The surfaces are uneven, featuring small pits, crevices, and angular edges typical of naturally occurring stones. The rocks are arranged in a loose, linear cluster, as if scattered or intentionally placed to form a low barrier. There are no visible markings, artificial modifications, or signs of integration with other materials.
description: A cluster of seven irregular, gray-brown rocks with rough, weathered surfaces and natural, angular forms. The rocks are arranged in a loose line, varying in size and shape.
description_view_0: A straight-on view showing all seven rocks in a row, highlighting their size differences and rough, granular surfaces.
description_view_1: An angled perspective emphasizing the depth and thickness of the rocks, with the tallest rock appearing more prominent.
description_view_2: A rear view displaying the rocks' irregular backs and the overall width of the cluster.
description_view_3: A side angle that reveals the varying heights and the uneven, jagged edges of the rocks.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped sidewalk edge
